plan: On backends without a native duration type, duration-only arithmetic returns a non-integer value: SQLite's django_format_dtdelta() returns str(timedelta) (e.g. '1 day, 0:00:00') and MySQL's DurationValue/date_interval_sql compiles the timedelta operand to a bare INTERVAL fragment that yields an unusable result, so convert_durationfield_value() in base/operations.py crashes because it assumes integer microseconds. Fix convert_durationfield_value() to also handle string results (falling back to django.utils.dateparse.parse_duration), and compile timedelta operands in DurationExpression as plain microsecond values via format_for_duration_arithmetic instead of the DurationValue/date_interval_sql INTERVAL path so both operands are microseconds on SQLite and MySQL. Add a regression test in tests/expressions/tests.py annotating F('estimated_time') + timedelta.
